findings.issue: T9 specifies parseUsdPrice('0') must throw (line 70 TDD anchor 'parseUsdPrice_rejects_zero', line 193 AC). T6 specifies parseUsdPrice('0') is valid and returns 0n (line 215 Edge cases: "parseUsdPrice('0') is valid → 0n. parseUsdPrice('0.0') is valid → 0n") and T6's regex /^\d+(\.\d{1,6})?$/ (line 66) accepts '0'. Implementations following T6 will fail T9's test. This is a direct cross-task contract contradiction.
findings.fix: Reconcile the two tasks. Either: (a) T6 updates the regex to /^(0*[1-9]\d*|0+(\.\d{0,5}[1-9])|\d+\.\d{1,6})$/ or adds an explicit value>0 check + updates Edge cases to say '0' is rejected; OR (b) T9 changes parseUsdPrice_rejects_zero to parseUsdPrice_accepts_zero and removes '0' from the rejection AC list at line 193. The systemic-fixes doc should also pin the canonical behavior so T6 and T9 stay aligned.

findings.issue: T9 line 151 places the chainId-pin throw test in core.test.ts and says 'core throws NetworkMismatchError on first request'. Per systemic-fix §5 and T7 (line 40, 84) and T8 (line 33), the chainId pin is performed in settle.ts; core.ts does NOT perform the chainId assertion itself. The throw originates from settle.ts, propagates through core. T7 already has the canonical assertion test at src/__tests__/settle.test.ts (line 84: 'chainId pin mismatches → NetworkMismatchError'). The core.test.ts test is fine as an integration check but the wording 'core throws' is misleading.
findings.fix: Rename the test to 'core surfaces NetworkMismatchError thrown by settle on first request' and clarify it asserts the propagation + the D18 chain_id_mismatch event emission (which IS core's responsibility per T8). Keep the canonical throw test in settle.test.ts.

findings.issue: T8's pipeline step 7e (line 55) handles classified settle failures ('On classified failure: return 402 settlement_failed with reason ...') but does not describe how core handles a thrown NetworkMismatchError from settle.ts. Per T7, settle throws NetworkMismatchError on chainId-pin mismatch — this is NOT one of the seven classified reasons; it is a hard throw. T9 expects core to emit a chain_id_mismatch SecurityLogger event in response. T8 should document the try/catch + event emission path so it is not missed by the implementer.
findings.fix: Add an explicit sub-step to T8's pipeline: 'On NetworkMismatchError from settle: emit securityEvent(chain_id_mismatch, {expectedChainId, observedChainId}) and either re-throw or return 402 with reason. Decide the surface behavior and pin it.'

round1_findings_resolved: T8 wave 7 + depends_on [6,7]; T7 wave 6 — strict wave > dependency wave preserved
T9 has no phantom test file creation for price.test, factory-cache.test, security-logger.test (only mentioned in negative form '— do NOT create those phantom files')
T7 settle.ts owns WalletClient; T8 core.ts owns PublicClient + NonceStore + factory-state cache — competing client cache resolved per systemic-fix §5 ownership table
T9 depends_on: [6, 7, 8] — adds T6 and T7 as required
T10 depends_on: [5, 8, 9] — adds T9 as required
T6 test directory canonicalized to packages/middleware/src/__tests__/ throughout (description, TDD anchors, AC, Details, Verification)
T6 arc-mainnet placeholder uses chainId 0 and id 'eip155:0' (verified in description, What-to-do, TDD anchor, AC)
T6 packages/middleware/package.json present in Files to modify (Description line 23 'this task ships', What-to-do line 94, Context Files line 177, Details line 202, AC line 156)
